what_it_does: Implements the map/assemble/reduce distill pipeline for recollection: reads raw exchange rows, maps each through an extractor to typed ExtractRecords, routes populated fields into either stored units (non-gated) or staged candidates (gated), and performs a thin deterministic reduce that clusters pattern units by exact pattern_tag string.
observations: The pipeline is explicitly shaped as MAP -> ASSEMBLE -> REDUCE, with the exchange riding inside each per-unit input.
Routing is registry-driven via getUnitType(...).gated rather than hardcoded kind checks.
Empty extract fields are skipped (no-fiction discipline) and counted in emptySkipped.
Deterministic unit ids are md5(sourceAddress|kind), making re-distillation idempotent via INSERT OR REPLACE.
The reduce step is model-free and only exact-matches pattern_tag strings; semantic clustering is intentionally deferred as DEPTH.
mapExchanges fails loud on ExtractError (served extractor transport) but collects per-item failures otherwise.
The whole assemble phase runs inside a single better-sqlite3 transaction.
readSourceExchanges falls back to row id when session_id is null for lineage/provenance parity.
standouts: The registry-driven gated/non-gated split is the central design hinge: principle goes to candidates, everything else self-assembles into units.
The deliberate vocabulary bridge between snake_case extract fields and kebab-case unit types is localized in FIELD_TO_UNIT_TYPE.
The default extractor path is a deterministic stub, so the harness loads no model by default.
conventions: Uses better-sqlite3 synchronous transactions wrapped around an async map step.
Idempotent identifiers via md5 of stable source address + kind.
Fail-loud for config/transport errors, soft per-item failure collection.
Comments are heavy and architectural, labeling each pipeline phase.
concerns: mapExchanges mixes async extraction with a synchronous db transaction only in assemble; transport errors thrown inside Promise.all could reject the whole run without partial results.
The fallback sessionId = r.session_id || r.id in readSourceExchanges may create lineage inconsistency if the same exchange later has a real session_id.
reducePatterns only clusters by exact string match, so near-duplicate tags remain separate until the flagged DEPTH semantic step.
assembleRecord calls getUnitType inside a tight loop; a missing kind will throw and rollback the entire transaction.
notes: This is the scaffolding slice of the distill pipeline: it wires extraction output into the recollection store with a clear gated-vs-unit split and leaves semantic rollup for a later DEPTH stage.
questions: Where is the extractor implementation and what does ExtractRecord look like exactly?
How are candidates promoted from 'proposed' to units, and which component performs ratification?
What does seedDistillUnitTypes register, and which kinds besides principle are gated?
Is the semantic embed-cluster (:8007/bge-m3) and L3 rollup implemented elsewhere in distill/index.ts?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose — a non-gated scaffolding map/assemble/reduce distill pipeline — matches the implementation; it deliberately does not implement the deeper semantic clustering or summarizer integration it references.
apparent_themes: Registry-driven gating instead of hardcoded kind branching
Fail-loud config errors vs soft per-item failures
Depth layering (thin deterministic core, semantic depth flagged elsewhere)
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a clean, auditable extraction-to-store pipeline where raw exchanges are mined into typed memory artifacts, with sensitive high-level abstractions held in a staging area for authority review before becoming stored units.
novelty: The explicit registry-gated self-assembly versus candidate-staging split, combined with deterministic idempotent ids and a localized vocabulary bridge between extract fields and unit types, is the distinctive design move.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, transactional pipeline shape that turns exchange captures into stored units and staged candidates while keeping the reduce step thin and deterministic, so downstream depth layers can build on stable, idempotent outputs.
summary_for_embedding: Map/assemble/reduce distill harness that extracts typed records from exchanges, writes non-gated fields as idempotent units and gated fields as proposed candidates, and clusters pattern units by exact tag.
intention_for_embedding: To scaffold a deterministic, model-free first cut of the recollection distill pipeline that separates self-assembling memory from gated abstractions awaiting ratification.
suggested_fields: depth_flags — to capture which functionality is intentionally deferred (e.g., semantic embed-cluster, L3 rollup)
gated_kinds — to record which unit types are staged as candidates
provenance_address_scheme — to document the exchange://<sid>/<lineStart> addressing convention
proposed_edge_kinds: stages — candidate 'proposed' status staged by this file for later ratification
defers_to — thin reduce defers semantic clustering to the DEPTH layer
bridges_vocabulary — FIELD_TO_UNIT_TYPE bridges extract field names to registry kinds

symbols.does: Imported typed shape of a per-exchange extraction record.
symbols.description: Represents the typed output of extractOne for a single exchange, with one field per extract category; the harness routes each populated field to a unit type.

symbols.does: Imported input shape for the extractor containing the exchange content.
symbols.description: Carries the user message, assistant response, session id, and timestamp for a single exchange; the harness builds one ExchangeUnit per source row.

symbols.does: Local interface for a raw exchange row read from the store.
symbols.description: Defines the fields the harness reads from the exchanges table, including lineage fields (sessionId, project, lineStart) used to build stable source addresses.

symbols.does: Pairs a source exchange with its extracted record.
symbols.description: The per-item output of the MAP step; used by assemble and reduce to route or cluster the extracted signals.

symbols.does: Thin reduce output grouping pattern units by exact tag.
symbols.description: Contains a pattern_tag string, the count of matching pattern units, and the list of unit ids that carry that tag.

symbols.does: Aggregated result of a full distill run.
symbols.description: Reports round id, extractor endpoint, stub flag, counts for scanned/mapped/assembled/candidate/empty, pattern clusters, and any per-item failures.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/distill/harness.ts::sourceAddressOf | function | export function sourceAddressOf(ex: SourceExchange): string {
symbols.does: Builds the canonical exchange://<sid>/<lineStart> provenance address for a source row.
symbols.description: Mirrors the db.provenanceAddress convention; uses sessionId or id as the session identifier and lineStart as the stable ordinal.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/distill/harness.ts::distilledId | function | export function distilledId(sourceAddress: string, kind: string): string {
symbols.does: Computes a deterministic md5 unit id from source address and kind.
symbols.description: Enables idempotent re-distillation: the same source and kind always produce the same id, so insertUnit/insertCandidate update in place rather than duplicating.

symbols.does: Converts a SourceExchange row into the extractor's ExchangeUnit input.
symbols.description: Maps userMessage/assistantMessage/sessionId/timestamp into the field names expected by extractOne.

symbols.does: Runs the extractor over all source exchanges concurrently.
symbols.description: Returns MapResult pairs; per-item extraction failures are collected, but ExtractError transport failures fail loud because they indicate a dead EXTRACT_URL configuration.

symbols.does: Routes each populated extract field to a unit or candidate based on registry gating.
symbols.description: Iterates EXTRACT_FIELDS, skips empty values, computes a deterministic id, and calls insertUnit for non-gated kinds or insertCandidate with status 'proposed' for gated kinds.

symbols.code_id: code://company/recollection/src/distill/harness.ts::reducePatterns | function | export function reducePatterns(mapped: MapResult[]): PatternCluster[] {
symbols.does: Clusters pattern units by exact pattern_tag string.
symbols.description: Builds a map from trimmed tag to the list of pattern unit ids, then returns sorted PatternCluster objects; it is model-free and deterministic.

symbols.does: Orchestrates the full MAP -> ASSEMBLE -> REDUCE pipeline in one transaction.
symbols.description: Seeds unit types, generates a round id, maps exchanges, assembles results inside a better-sqlite3 transaction, reduces patterns, and returns a DistillResult.

symbols.does: Reads raw exchange rows from the exchanges table for a distill run.
symbols.description: Supports optional project/sessionId/limit filters, orders by timestamp, and normalizes nullable session_id to id for lineage parity.
